metadata.notes: Frozen-claim verification-only replay. Claim set and contexts are held fixed; only the verifier model varies. No gold labels — agreement and support-rate are judge-vs-judge metrics, not accuracy metrics.

A synthetic long put option can be created by combining a short stock position with a long call option.
This strategy allows you to profit as the stock price decreases while limiting your risk if the stock price rises.

gpt_4o.evidence: A long put - you have a small initial cost (the option premium) but profit as the stock goes down. You have no additional risk if the shock rises, even a lot.

gpt_4o_mini.evidence: A long put - you have a small initial cost (the option premium) but profit as the stock goes down. You have no additional risk if the shock rises, even a lot.

results.claim: The profit/loss graph for this combination resembles that of a long put when both positions are held.
